note: Complete reference examples showing the quality and specificity expected for all 95 routes in the watch_out research project

exampleRoutes.name: Freedom or Death
watch_out: Washington Pass avalanche slope exposure on approach; assess snow stability carefully April-June; generally stable July-September
Runout 5.10 slab pitch (pitch 3) with 30+ feet of exposure; loose rock quality; poor protection placement; requires excellent footwork and confidence
Crux mixed terrain transitions (5.7 rock to water ice) on pitches 4-5; limited belay anchors between pitch sections; verify all anchors
Descent route-finding critical; multiple gully options confuse parties; study descent photos/topo before climbing; wrong turns common
Rockfall hazard mid-afternoon as sun melts frozen rock sections; popular route with crowding; early start (dawn) essential; expect other parties
Weather deteriorates rapidly after 1pm; afternoon thunderstorms common June-August; morning-only summits strongly recommended
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: High-exposure rock/ice with avalanche risk on approach, runout slabs, descent route-finding complexity, weather exposure

exampleRoutes.name: Northwest Face (Boving-Pollock)
watch_out: Sparse protection quality on upper pitches; 5.11 climbing with 20-30 feet between marginal pieces; runout protection sections; high consequence for falls
Snow moat guards base of route through mid-July; may require 4th class scrambling or short ice climbing (WI2-WI3) to bypass; bring ice screws
Rappel descent requires 2-3 rappels with marginal anchor quality; fixed slings often weathered and worn; carry personal anchor material; verify systems
Exposed climbing throughout all pitches; northwest face exposure to afternoon weather; afternoon thunderstorms possible mid-July through August
Route-finding on descent complex with multiple anchor stations; practice rappel sequences; know exact descent line before deploying
Loose rock hazard in sections; careful footwork on approach and early pitches; alert for rockfall from upper parties
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Sparse protection, snow moat, complex rappel descent, exposed weather, route-finding complexity, loose rock

exampleRoutes.name: Upper North Ridge w/Great Gendarme
watch_out: North Face avalanche slope exposure; significant serac formation and slide potential April-July; assess snow stability and weather carefully; generally safe August-September
Great Gendarme crux (5.9+ mixed rock/ice) has 20+ feet of exposure with sparse protection options; fall zone significant; requires solid mixed technique
Complex descent route-finding with multiple party reversals reported near summit; many gully junctions; cairns unreliable; study descent before climb
Long commitment; 8-10 hours round trip from base; turnaround time critical; conservative descent decision-making essential for darkness buffer
Afternoon thunderstorms common August; weather deteriorates rapidly after 1pm on exposed ridge; morning-only summit ascents strongly recommended
Altitude and fitness; high altitude (11,000+ ft) with significant elevation gain; proper acclimatization and fitness important
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Avalanche exposure, mixed terrain crux, complex descent, high commitment, afternoon weather, altitude exposure

exampleRoutes.name: Beckey-Davis
watch_out: Mixed terrain transitions (5.7 rock to 3rd class scrambling) with variable belay quality; some pitches lack adequate belay anchors; verify anchor placement
Loose rock common throughout route; careful footwork and block testing required; alert for rockfall from other parties above; defensive positioning important
Route-finding on both ascent and descent important; multiple options and junctions; cairns unreliable; practice route navigation mentally before climbing
Altitude and exertion; Prusik Peak approach from Colchuck Lake base camp is high and committing; proper acclimatization and fitness critical
Weather exposure on west-facing terrain; afternoon storms and thermal winds common; morning ascents strongly recommended; weather deteriorates rapidly
Descent route-finding as important as ascent; many party reversals; verify route and cairn system; practice downclimbing before committing
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Mixed terrain with poor belays, loose rock, route-finding complexity, altitude exposure, afternoon weather

exampleRoutes.name: Czech it Out
watch_out: Loose blocky terrain on approach and first pitch; dislodge carefully and alert for rockfall from other parties; defensive positioning important
Crumbly rock quality with 25+ feet of runout between protection points on pitch 1; requires solid footwork, confidence on 5.8 climbing, and good placing
Afternoon thunderstorms common July-August; exposed terrain; afternoon weather window; start climbing by 7am for safe descent before 2pm
Descent route-finding required; multiple gully options in area; cairns unreliable; study descent before climbing; practice route mentally
Snow/ice possible upper pitches through June; ice axe and crampons may be needed for season safety; check seasonal conditions
Popular area with crowding; many parties on route; rockfall from other parties possible; stay alert and position defensively
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Loose rock, runout slab with poor protection, afternoon weather, route-finding complexity, seasonal ice

exampleRoutes.name: South Face
watch_out: Weather exposure on south-facing wall; afternoon sun thermal winds and temperature changes; afternoon thunderstorms July-August; start climbing by 7am
Route-finding on descent critical; multiple gully options and ridge variations; cairns unreliable; study descent route before climbing
Loose rock quality in sections of climbing; careful footwork required; dislodge blocks carefully; alert for rockfall from other parties
Snow/ice possible on upper pitches through June; ice axe and crampons recommended for season safety; check conditions before committing
Commitment level moderate to high; 6-8 hours round trip including descent; turnaround time critical for darkness buffer; be aggressive with retreat
Popular area with crowding; multiple parties on route; rockfall from upper parties possible; stay aware and position defensively
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Weather exposure, descent route-finding, loose rock, seasonal ice, moderate commitment, crowding hazards

exampleRoutes.name: Dolphin Chimney
watch_out: Chimney crux (5.9+) with variable rock quality; some sections have hollow sounds and loose blocks; careful footwork and body positioning required
Protection sparse in chimney sections; requires good placing ability and confidence; fall consequence significant; test all blocks before weight
Scree descent following climb is loose and prone to rockfall; other parties' rockfall hazard significant; descend carefully and separately from others
Snow/ice possible on approach through July; ice climbing (WI2) may be required to bypass snow moats; ice screws recommended for season safety
Weather deteriorates mid-afternoon; afternoon thunderstorms common mid-July through August; early morning start essential for safety
Route-finding on descent important; scree gully system has multiple branches; cairns unreliable; study descent line before committing to climb
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: Chimney crux with variable rock, sparse protection, loose scree descent, seasonal ice, afternoon weather, route-finding

exampleRoutes.name: Gorillas in the Mist
watch_out: High-grade mixed terrain (5.11- rock/ice) with limited protection placements; significant exposure on several pitches; fall zones substantial
Serac exposure on approach and route; assess hazards carefully; July-September window safest; June conditions require careful assessment
Complex route-finding with many junctions and options; wrong turns extend route time significantly; study route photos and topo carefully
Long multi-pitch commitment; 10-12 hours round trip; turnaround time critical; be aggressive with retreat decisions; darkness buffer essential
Weather exposure on high altitude; afternoon thunderstorms common August; morning-only summit ascents strongly recommended; weather deteriorates rapidly
Altitude (11,000+ ft) with significant commitment; acclimatization and fitness important; descend carefully on long descent
exampleRoutes.hazardSummary: High-grade mixed with limited protection, serac exposure, complex route-finding, long commitment, altitude exposure

keyPatterns: Each watch_out entry is specific with location details (pitch number, feature name, etc.)
Entries describe actual hazard and how to manage it (not just 'be careful')
Seasonal variations are noted (June-July, mid-July, July-September, etc.)
Measurable details provided (distance/feet, hours, time of day)
Life-threatening hazards prioritized (protection, falls, weather, avalanche)
Route-specific knowledge evident (chimney type, rock quality, gully system, etc.)
Average 4-6 hazards per route, covering all major categories
No duplicate or generic hazards across similar routes
commonHazardPhrases: Sparse protection quality with 20-30 feet between pieces
Loose rock quality; careful footwork and alert for rockfall
Descent route-finding critical; study before climbing
Weather deteriorates rapidly after [time]; morning ascents recommended
Snow moat through [month]; may require ice climbing to bypass
Exposed terrain; minimize time on route

quality_checklist.actionability: How to manage hazard, not just warning
